title: Belgaum Fort
tagline: Belgaum Fort — heritage city of Karnataka

overview.short: Belgaum Fort is in the city of Belgaum, in the Belgaum district, in Karnataka state, India.
overview.description: Belgaum Fort is in the city of Belgaum, in the Belgaum district, in Karnataka state, India. It was begun by Jaya Raya, also called Bichi Raja, an ally of the Ratta dynasty, in the year 1204. It has undergone several renovations over the centuries under dynastic rulers of the region. The fort, built with ramparts and a large moat, with historical and religious monuments dated to the Adil Shahi dynasty.

topPlaces.name: Kamal Basadi
topPlaces.description: Kamal Basadi is a Jain temple located inside Belgaum Fort in city of Belgaum, Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Shahapur, Belgaum
topPlaces.description: Shahapur is a village in Belgaum district of Karnataka, India. It is now a suburb of Belgaum city.

topPlaces.name: Diocese of Belgaum
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Belgaum (Latin: Belgaumen(sis)) is a diocese located in the city of Belgaum in the ecclesiastical province of Bangalore in India.

topPlaces.name: Belgaum Cantonment
topPlaces.description: Belgaum Cantonment is a cantonment in Belgaum district in the state of Karnataka, India.

topPlaces.name: Belagavi division
topPlaces.description: Belagavi division, formerly Belgaum division, is one of the four divisions of Indian state of Karnataka. The region is also called as Kitturu Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Belgaum
topPlaces.description: Belgaum (Kannada ISO: Beḷagāvi, IPA: [beɭɐɡaːʋi]; Marathi ISO: Bēḻagāṃva, IPA: [beɭɡaːũ]), officially known as Belagavi (also Belgaon), is a city in the Indian state of Karnataka located near its northern western border in the Western Ghats. It is the administrative headquarters of the eponymous Belagavi division and Belagavi district.

topPlaces.name: Kangrali (KH)
topPlaces.description: Kangrali (KH) is a census town in Belgaum district in the Indian state of Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Halabhavi
topPlaces.description: Halabhavi is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Belgaum taluk of Belgaum district in Karnataka.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Belgaum Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Belgaum Fort is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Belgaum Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Belagavi Airport (10 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (87 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Belgaum Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Belgaum Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Belgaum Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Belgaum Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Belgaum Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kamal Basadi, Shahapur, Belgaum, Diocese of Belgaum, Belgaum Cantonment, Belagavi division.
